# UC-19.1.23 · Intersight Audit Log and Configuration Changes

## Description

Tracks every admin action and policy modification in Intersight. Correlating these with incident timelines reveals whether infrastructure changes contributed to outages or security events.

## Value

Tracks every admin action and policy modification in Intersight. Correlating these with incident timelines reveals whether infrastructure changes contributed to outages or security events.

## Implementation

Ingest audit logs every 5 minutes. Alert on high-impact changes (profile deployments, firmware policy changes) outside change windows. Feed into ES notable events for SOC visibility.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco Intersight Add-on`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `cisco:intersight:auditRecords`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest audit logs every 5 minutes. Alert on high-impact changes (profile deployments, firmware policy changes) outside change windows. Feed into ES notable events for SOC visibility.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:auditRecords" earliest=-24h
| where action IN ("Update","Delete","Create") AND object_type IN ("server.Profile","firmware.Policy","ntp.Policy","boot.PrecisionPolicy")
| stats count by user_email, action, object_type, object_name
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Intersight Audit Log and Configuration Changes** — Tracks every admin action and policy modification in Intersight. Correlating these with incident timelines reveals whether infrastructure changes contributed to outages or security events.

Documented **Data sources**: `cisco:intersight:auditRecords`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco Intersight Add-on`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_intersight; **sourcetype**: cisco:intersight:auditRecords. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cisco_intersight, sourcetype="cisco:intersight:auditRecords", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where action IN ("Update","Delete","Create") AND object_type IN ("server.Profile","firmware.Policy","ntp.Policy","boot.Prec…` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user_email, action, object_type, object_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Intersight Audit Log and Configuration Changes** — Tracks every admin action and policy modification in Intersight

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Change.All_Changes` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Cisco Intersight (Alarms, audit, and inventory) for the same object types and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (recent changes), Timeline (change events), Bar chart (changes by user).

## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:auditRecords" earliest=-24h
| where action IN ("Update","Delete","Create") AND object_type IN ("server.Profile","firmware.Policy","ntp.Policy","boot.PrecisionPolicy")
| stats count by user_email, action, object_type, object_name
| sort -count
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (recent changes), Timeline (change events), Bar chart (changes by user).
